data.description:
# Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core

## Why

The gates need a real on-disk tree at a pinned SHA, but the server's worktree is mutable and
shared. `git archive` is lossy (drops `export-ignore` files, omits submodules, returns LFS
pointer files, rewrites `export-subst`) â€” unacceptable for an attestation that claims "this
is the code at SHA X". We need a faithful, concurrency-safe, portable materialization.

## What

A core module (e.g. `src/rebar/_engine/repo_snapshot.py`) exposing `materialize(ref, *,
source_mode) -> handle` (context-managed):
- Coalesced `git fetch origin` (the only lock-taking step); resolve `ref` (branch|tag|SHA)
  to an immutable SHA against origin.
- FAITHFUL tree materialization: `git read-tree <sha>` into a temp index (`GIT_INDEX_FILE`)
  then `git checkout-index --all --prefix=<tmp>/` â€” exact committed tree, no worktree, no
  `export-ignore` drops.
- Atomic population: build into `<root>/tmp/<uuid>/`, fsync files, `rename()` the directory
  into the cache path, fsync parent dir; readers see all-or-nothing.
- Portability: extraction never shells to external `tar`; temp root from
  `tempfile.gettempdir()`/`mkdtemp` (0700), overridable via `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR`, created
  OUTSIDE the repo/.git; `fcntl.flock` with atomic-`mkdir` fallback on a local FS.
- Descriptive errors: a fetch that fails on auth surfaces an actionable credential message
  (see S6), not a raw git error; attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs.
- Fetch prerequisites documented: arbitrary-SHA fetch needs `uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant`
  (or fetch a containing ref then resolve); prefer `--filter=blob:none` over deep shallow refetch.

## Scope

- In: the materialize() core, faithful tree extraction, atomic population, portability seam,
  fetch-coalescing, descriptive fetch/auth errors.
- Out: the cache/janitor (S2), gate wiring (S3), signing (S4), surfaces (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `materialize(ref)` produces a directory whose contents byte-match the committed tree at
      the resolved SHA, INCLUDING `export-ignore`-marked files (faithful, not `git archive`).
- [ ] LFS pointer files are detected (not silently served as content); submodule handling is
      defined and documented.
- [ ] Population is atomic: a reader never observes a partial tree; a crashed materialization
      leaves only a `tmp/` dir, cleaned on startup.
- [ ] No external `tar` binary is invoked; no hardcoded `/tmp`; runs on macOS + Linux.
- [ ] A private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error;
      attested mode fails closed.
- [ ] Concurrent `materialize()` of different SHAs do not contend on `index.lock`/`config.lock`.
data.title: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core
